reviews.review_description:
I like the idea of seeing the 'ghost' avatars to click on to teleport around. One thing that could be improved is to let the targeting laser go through objects, so you can still teleport if a booth is in the way. Inside the train car, I found that I couldn't teleport to a slot that I could see, because my laser was blocked by a booth.

I would also like a way to play seated. Need a snap turn function.

The lighting was a bit 'off' for me. Not really sure what it needed, but something wasn't quite 'there' for me. Maybe it didn't feel there was light coming from anywhere inside the car itself? I'm not sure.

developer_response.body: thanks for the feedback! please re-center your view frequently and you should have no problem using the experience sitting.  i hear you on the lighting, and what we were trying to do is simulate real life conditions of being in a train cabin at sunset without any lights on in the cabin. it is intentionally dim in order to be easier on your eyes and facilitate relaxation. however, in the Quest version we will be working a lot on the lighting and allow you to turn on lamps and stuff.

reviews.review_title: best in the dark with headphones!
reviews.review_description: thanks for trying my first VR app! it is best enjoyed in the dark with good headphones 🙏🏾🙏🏾

...I like the real time lighting! 

only thing putting me of is that the scale of the objects like seats, table, trees is too big; it destroys immersion, because realistic scale is key, even when the graphics are stylized. 

A snapturn obtion would be nice for playing on the couch. I would love to have free roam. are you restricted by performance, because collision detection must be calculated additionally?

developer_response.body: the interior of the train cabin is indeed taller than any train you'll find IRL, however aside from that special care was given to the scale of objects throughout the experience, and should feel accurate for most people. glad you found something in it you enjoyed!

reviews.review_description: Really nice, genuinely relaxing experience. Loved the default seat in front of the radio with the third station on. The colours and general ambience was great. Improvements i would suggest would be more ambient music to choose from and some life in the outside environment (the odd animal or person). Can see this being one of my favourite apps to chill to and hope updates and improvements continue on the Go version.

developer_response.body: thank you so much and i appreciate that you understand what we were doing with this app. An oculus quest version is planned that will have the same updates you are suggesting 🙏🏿

reviews.review_description: It's an enjoyable and relaxing ride and it's free. Download it! The idea has interesting potential and I'd like to see it developed into something a bit more complex.

developer_response.body: thanks for the feedback! we would love to see it develop into something more as well, and we are adding additional content to the Oculus quest version in progress now. we hope that there is enough here on Go to create a sense of place and allow for a relaxing journey watching the world pass by.

reviews.review_description:
It's definitely different from what I'm used to seeing on the Go.  However, I think it would've been better if it was in the daytime.  This way, you could get to see the pretty graphics.  It was just a little too red and dark for me, or perhaps you can keep it in the night time mode but take out the red & replace it with realistic colors that you find inside the train and change the sky to a night blue, just more realistic colors.  I personally thought the app was very cool.. but would love to see it in the daytime too.

Also, I was very impressed on how you made the GO controller work like the Quest controller.  You could actually pick up items on the train just from the remote.  For a minute there, I thought I was using the Quest.

developer_response.body: thanks and I'm glad you enjoyed it! the Go is limited in it's lighting capabilities, but in the quest version we plan on making the lamps functional as well as implementing a day & night lighting cycle outside which should really add a lot 🙏🏿

reviews.review_description: Theres not much space and u wish you could go to the other parts of the train and could you please make a rc train that is exactly like the train where it has passenger carts and a controller to drive it and when your driving it then you go into the rc train in first person.

developer_response.body: thank you for the suggestions! there are a lot of hidden and hard to reach teleport points on the experience, you can ride the front of the train, just above the tracks! happy exploring.

reviews.review_title: Not Bad - but not my cup of tea.
reviews.review_description:
This is a rather charming idea, an app that simulates a train journey, but it has a few issues that bothered me.
I'm not a fan of "teleport travel" in VR (I get that it helps some, but I find it disimmersing), and the wire-work ghosts of "teleport points" I find a little off-putting. Also, the fact that you can only explore one cabin bothers me, since I like the idea of exploring the whole train. Plus, besides one or two notes, there's nothing really to do in the cabin. I understand that the point is the journey, but I didn't really enjoy the view out the windows. And besides those wire-work ghosts, there are no other passengers on the train, which I personally find a little eerie, but I can see how, if you wanted to relax, the solitude would be a feature, not a flaw.
So, I must admit that a lot of this is personal preference. On a less sour note - the fact that the train travels, and occasionally stops at stations is quite fun. And although I didn't like it, I must admit the aesthetic is consistent throughout the train and countryside. The ambient sound of the train noise was well done (it even gets louder as you head out of the carriage), so if you are looking for the atmosphere of a passenger train, you will find it here.

All in all, I did not enjoy this app, but I can understand it's appeal, and if you aren't bothered by the issues that bothered me, then do yourself a favour, and download this now.

developer_response.body: hi and thanks for taking the time to try out our experience. you mentioned you wish you could explore the train and find more to do. I just wanted to point out that you can go to the front train engine, stand on top of the train, even sit at the front watching the tracks. look up on the train's roof when you are on the back deck of the train. also, there is a guided meditation on the radio which you can follow along with. 🙏🏼
